description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: neighborhood in the San Fernando Valley in Los Angeles, California, United States
city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.

description: country in Eastern Europe and Northern Asia
description_wiki: Russia, or the Russian Federation, is a country in Eastern Europe and North Asia.

description: country in north-west Europe, part of the United Kingdom
description_wiki: England is a country that is part of the United Kingdom.

description: seat of San Diego County, California, United States; second-largest city in California
description_wiki: San Diego (  SAN dee-AY-goh; Spanish: [san ˈdjeɣo]) is a city on the Pacific coast of Southern California, adjacent to the Mexico–United States border.

description: city in Los Angeles County, California, United States
description_wiki: Culver City is a city in Los Angeles County, California, United States.

description: neighborhood in Los Angeles, California, USA
description_wiki: Palms  (originally "The Palms") is a community in the Westside region of Los Angeles, California, founded in 1886 and the oldest neighborhood annexed to the city, in 1915.

description: public research university in Reno, Nevada, United States
description_wiki: The University of Nevada, Reno (Nevada, the University of Nevada, or UNR) is a public land-grant research university in Reno, Nevada, United States.

description: reserve force of the United States Army and Air Force

aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

aliases: U.S. Military Academy at West Point
United States Military Academy at West Point
description: federal service academy in West Point, New York, United States
description_wiki: The United States Military Academy (USMA), commonly known as West Point, is a United States service academy in West Point, New York, that educates cadets for service as commissioned officers in the United States Army.

description: air service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Air Force (USAF) is the air service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: closed United States Air Force Base
description_wiki: Mather Air Force Base (Mather AFB) was a United States Air Force Base, which was closed in 1993 pursuant to a post-Cold War BRAC decision.

description: training aircraft family by Stearman, a subsidiary of Boeing
state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Louisiana (French: Louisiane; Spanish: Luisiana [lwiˈsjana]; Louisiana Creole: Lwizyàn) is a state in the Deep South and South Central regions of the United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Texas (  TEK-səss) is the most populous state in the Southern United States.

description: city in Riverside County, California, United States
description_wiki: Blythe () is a city in eastern Riverside County, California, United States.

description: American military officer and aviation pioneer (1896–1993)
description_wiki: James Harold "Jimmy" Doolittle (December 14, 1896 – September 27, 1993) was an American military general and aviation pioneer who received the Medal of Honor for his raid on Japan during World War II, known as the Doolittle Raid in his honor.

description: first air raid on the Japanese mainland conducted by the U.S. military on April 18, 1942
description_wiki: The Doolittle Raid (also known as Doolittle's Raid or the Tokyo Raid) was an air raid by the United States on the Japanese capital of Tokyo and other places in Honshu during World War II. Launched on 18 April 1942, it was the first American air operation to strike the Japanese archipelago.

description: military unit
description_wiki: The 91st Bombardment Group (Heavy) was an air combat unit of the United States Army Air Forces during the Second World War.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki:
Florida (  FLORR-ih-də; Spanish: [floˈɾiða] ) is a state in the Southeastern and South Atlantic
regions of the United States.

description: city in Walla Walla County, Washington, United States
description_wiki: Walla Walla ( WAH-lə WAH-lə) is a city in and the county seat of Walla Walla County, Washington, United States.
